name: Dr. Mar Theophilus School - Dhanori

address.Address: Sr no.14/1, Porwal Rd, Next to Regency Meadows, DN Parande Park, Dhanori, Pune, Maharashtra 411015

address.streetAddress: Sr no.14/1, Porwal Rd, Next to Regency Meadows, DN Parande Park, Dhanori, Pune, Maharashtra 411015

itemListElement.reviewBody:
This can be regarded as a testimonial for the support and guidance given by each and every staff (teaching as well as non teaching). I have been associated for the past 5-6 years with this school and it reminds me of my school and the amount of discipline expected from students and indirectly from parents too. 
If you are looking for a school which will preach moral values, survival skills, discipline along with the curriculum then this is the right place to go. My experience has been golden with the Principal, various teachers and non teaching staff who propagate positivity and a willingness to assist your child in times of need. I thank you for that. 
It is crucial to have good teachers these days who don't take teaching as a job and realize that it is a selfless thankless deed and still continue teaching kids. My ward has found such stars here from Sophia mam in Nursery to Supriya Bali Mam, Shraddha Kamble Mam, Kamla Mam, Vellangani Mam, Anagha Mam, Anamika mam and many more in the Primary section.  Sophia mam who motivates the child to speak in English, Supriya mam who instills discipline and organization skills, Shraddha mam who encourages the child to get back on their feet after a setback, Kamla Mam who increases the curiosity level in science while being calm, Anagha Mam who will stand by the child while the child copes with weak points in English, Anamika mam who reminds the child to be positive in all situations, Vellangani Mam who supervises and reminds the child of what is right and what is wrong in the moment. As a parent it puts me in a tight schedule to make my ward develop under the shade of this giant tree. But I am happy to do what's best for my child.
Now as my ward moves to the Middle School she takes the treasures from all these teachers and aspires to become what she dreams of.
Thank you to all and keep motivating more generations.

I am not disclosing my name since I do not want my child to be tortured in School. Please do not put your child in this school. If you are looking for an English medium school,  then this is not the right place. Though they claim to follow the ICSE curriculum, the teachers do not teach in English. Hindi and Marathi is frequently spoken.The medium of communication is Malayalam,  Hindi and Marathi.

This is the worst school in Pune.The principal and the management are least bothered for the children's education. Teachers are not qualified to teach ICSE curriculum. English department is a disaster. The HOD is a B.A. (online) and has no knowledge to teach the ICSE curriculum. The Maths teachers are also not qualified. Geography department is also in a mess.Pre-primary teachers are appointed to teach Geography in class 9.All the students have to go to coaching classes to clear their doubts. Take admission at your own risk. 



 - pre- primary teachers are teaching Geography to class 9.

I am writing this review after 4 years of schooling of my daughter at Dr. Mar Theophilus School starting from nursery.

I am very grateful to all teachers and Principal Maam for all the efforts taken by them to provide a nurturing and enriching learning environment for students. Dr. Mar Theophilus School is maintaining good discipline. Yes Academics are important and it plays a crucial role in shaping a child and it provides students with a deeper understanding of various subjects, develops critical thinking skills, prepares them for higher education.
Alongwith that, Extra-curricular activities are also highly appreciated, every month there are activities scheduled for child to participate and this opportunity builds confidence.

Special thanks to class teacher Supriya Bali maam, my daughters class teacher. Her dedication and hard work is a motivation to us as parents too. She is not just an educator to our child but also a mentor and role model. There were times when my daughter came home and reminded us of good habits and manners which maam taught children in the class and we all inculcated it in our family. Due to her teaching method and engaging children in studies, she has been such a positive influence on so many students from Class 1A. We still remember the first day of orientation and we understood that Supriya Bali maam is a very strict teacher, infact many parents said so but later we realised that she is perfect for our kids. In PTM also, it is commendable that she answers all the questions of all parents and leaves no room for any doubt. Its such a blessing to us that our kids have started eating full tiffin in school and they have started following good eating habits. If any child is absent, maam takes effort to get the notes completed next day itself so that the child is not burdened with the missed class work.
We are so grateful to have Supriya Bali maam as our daughter's class teacher. Maam's passion for teaching shines through in everything she does for her children, and it has had a significant impact on Khushi's academic growth this year.
She has excelled in Spell Bee and also in various competition that school conducted. She is now more confident and excited to learn new things.

We truly appreciate the entire staff and management team for always being approachable, courteous & professional. Our child's association with Dr. Mar Theophilus School is helping in building a strong foundation and we are positive that it will give her wings to fly in the future.
Nice school in pune. I like it😊😇

"First impression is the last impression!!"

Such a pathetic situation to be in for an institution. Its the admission time and the person holding mobile number that is listed on the website doesn't even care to pick up and help the parents with their queries to fill the form!! On top of this, the landline number is found to be a wrong/invalid number.

This shows how professional a school management can be.. happening this at the beginning.. i wonder what happens to the parents whose ward is studying there and they want to contact the school.. all their efforts go unanswered.

If the school management is reading this, please help!!

I don't know if it's a good school or not. However I stay in a nearby society. Since last week (26th or 27-Nov-2024), their Annual day practice is going on. I totally understand that they need to practice. However I don't understand why they have to do it on full volume. They can very well do it on low volume. They start at around 7:30 am and continue till 2 or 2:30 pm. I know all the songs they are going to present this year.
I work from home and this loud noise is a huge inconvenience for me & may be for many other people who stay nearby.

So there are 2 things I definitely know about this school:
(a) They might be teaching the kids about Sound pollution, how is it bad for us & how we should not contribute to it etc. But when it comes to practicing it themselves, they are contributing to it every year & causing inconvenience to the people staying nearby. (b) They lack basic common sense that we should always try to avoid causing inconvenience to other people because of our actions

I really hope that either the Principal or some other sensible teacher of this school reads this review and stop these practice sessions on loud music. On Annual day, they can very well keep the volume high. It will still be an inconvenience but can be managed for a day. Hope for the best (fingers crossed)
It is a very good school for children
No management, no etiquettes, just illogical rules in the name of strict standards.

Very pathetic management, went to enquire for the admission as we have just moved from abroad, the watchman won't even let us meet the counseller,  everyone talking in Marathi or either Hindi...is it seriously a english school we wonder ....
